main_sample_finding: Age was associated with higher odds of Sexual initiation (OR = 1.33, 95 percent CI [1.19; 1.50], p < 0.001).
sensitivity_sample_finding: Age was associated with higher odds of Sexual initiation (OR = 1.35, 95 percent CI [1.20; 1.53], p < 0.001).
public_interpretation: Age and grade behave as developmental markers associated with sexual initiation.

main_sample_finding: Age was associated with lower odds of Birth control use at most recent sex (OR = 0.84, 95 percent CI [0.72; 0.98], p = 0.031). Age was associated with higher odds of Ever used a condom during sex (OR = 1.36, 95 percent CI [1.00; 1.85], p = 0.050).
sensitivity_sample_finding: Age was associated with higher odds of Ever used a condom during sex (OR = 1.40, 95 percent CI [1.02; 1.94], p = 0.041).
public_interpretation: Protection-related outcomes should be interpreted separately; they do not form one uniform empirical pattern.

main_sample_finding: Age was associated with higher odds of Ever been pregnant (OR = 1.95, 95 percent CI [1.45; 2.62], p < 0.001).
sensitivity_sample_finding: Age was associated with higher odds of Ever been pregnant (OR = 1.85, 95 percent CI [1.36; 2.52], p < 0.001).
public_interpretation: Pregnancy history is strongly age-graded among eligible respondents, but skip patterns matter.

main_sample_finding: Age was associated with higher odds of Self-reported chlamydia diagnosis (OR = 1.58, 95 percent CI [1.06; 2.35], p = 0.024). Age was associated with higher odds of STD testing or treatment (OR = 1.61, 95 percent CI [1.30; 1.98], p < 0.001).
sensitivity_sample_finding: Age was associated with higher odds of STD testing or treatment (OR = 1.63, 95 percent CI [1.29; 2.05], p < 0.001).
public_interpretation: STI-related outcomes are sensitive to rarity, diagnosis type and service-use interpretation.

main_sample_finding: Family, school, peer and psychosocial levels remain theoretically central, but are treated as exploratory in this public report.
sensitivity_sample_finding: The same conservative interpretation rule is applied to the sensitivity sample.
public_interpretation: The report does not reject the socioecological model; it distinguishes final stable results from exploratory multilevel evidence.
